analyses.title: Medical Record Abstraction for Quality Improvement in Sepsis Care Using Artificial Intelligence: A Cluster Randomized Trial.
analyses.oneLineSummary: Near-real-time LLM-generated SEP-1 feedback to ED physicians improved sepsis bundle compliance from 70.1% to 82.9% without changing mortality or ICU admissions.
analyses.oneLineSummary_ko: ED 의사에게 제공된 거의 실시간 LLM 기반 SEP-1 피드백은 패혈증 번들 준수율을 70.1%에서 82.9%로 향상시켰으나 사망률이나 ICU 입원에는 변화가 없었다.
analyses.whyItMatters: SEP-1 quality reporting is labor-intensive and delayed, so timely AI-driven chart abstraction with physician-level feedback could close the gap between measurement and behavior change—but this trial shows a process improvement without a demonstrated patient outcome benefit.
analyses.whyItMatters_ko: SEP-1 품질 보고는 노동 집약적이고 지연되기 때문에, 의사 개인별 피드백을 동반한 적시의 AI 기반 차트 추출은 측정과 행동 변화 사이의 간극을 좁힐 수 있다. 다만 본 연구는 환자 결과 개선이 입증되지 않은 과정 지표 개선만을 보여준다.
analyses.clinicalQuestion: Does near-real-time, LLM-determined SEP-1 compliance feedback to emergency physicians improve adherence to the CMS Severe Sepsis and Septic Shock Management Bundle compared with standard quality feedback?
analyses.clinicalQuestion_ko: 응급의학과 의사에게 거의 실시간으로 제공되는 LLM 기반 SEP-1 준수 피드백이 표준 품질 피드백에 비해 CMS 중증 패혈증 및 패혈성 쇼크 관리 번들 준수율을 개선하는가?

studyDetails.design: Single-blind, unstratified, cluster randomized trial in which the cluster unit was the attending physician; effect sizes estimated with a mixed-effects logistic regression model (intervention as fixed effect, random intercept for physician). The trial registry (NCT07581340) lists masking as none and design as randomized.
studyDetails.design_ko: 단일맹검, 비층화 군집 무작위 배정 시험으로 군집 단위는 담당 전문의였다; 효과 크기는 혼합효과 로지스틱 회귀모형(중재를 고정효과, 의사를 무작위 절편으로)으로 추정되었다. 시험 등록부(NCT07581340)에는 맹검 없음, 무작위 배정 설계로 기재되어 있다.
studyDetails.setting: 2 academic emergency departments within the University of California, San Diego (UCSD) Health system, United States; conducted December 13, 2024 to July 8, 2025 (ClinicalTrials.gov NCT07581340, status COMPLETED).
studyDetails.setting_ko: 미국 캘리포니아 대학교 샌디에이고(UCSD) 헬스 시스템 내 2개 학술 응급실; 2024년 12월 13일부터 2025년 7월 8일까지 시행(ClinicalTrials.gov NCT07581340, 상태 COMPLETED).
studyDetails.sampleSize: 66 attending physicians randomized (clusters), who treated 301 SEP-1-eligible patients analyzed: 121 in the control group and 180 in the intervention group. The registry-reported actual enrollment of 66 refers to physicians, not patients.
studyDetails.sampleSize_ko: 66명의 담당 전문의가 무작위 배정됨(군집), 이들이 치료한 SEP-1 대상 환자 301명이 분석됨: 대조군 121명, 중재군 180명. 등록부에 보고된 실제 등록 66명은 환자가 아닌 의사를 의미한다.
studyDetails.eligibility: Clusters: all 66 attending physicians practicing in UCSD EDs working >3 shifts/month before study start. Patients: adults (≥18 years) evaluated in the ED with clinical severe sepsis or septic shock meeting CMS SEP-1 inclusion criteria, with sepsis time zero during the ED visit. Excluded: sepsis onset before ED arrival or after admission, insufficient documentation, transfers with sepsis care already initiated, and cases whose attending was not assigned to a study arm.
studyDetails.eligibility_ko: 군집: 연구 시작 전 월 3교대 이상 근무하며 UCSD 응급실에서 진료한 전체 66명 담당 전문의. 환자: ED에서 평가된 성인(≥18세)으로 임상적 중증 패혈증 또는 패혈성 쇼크가 있고 CMS SEP-1 포함 기준을 충족하며 패혈증 시점(time zero)이 ED 방문 중 발생. 제외: ED 도착 전 또는 입원 후 패혈증 발병, 문서 불충분, 이미 패혈증 치료가 시작된 전원 환자, 담당 의사가 연구군에 배정되지 않은 경우.
studyDetails.interventionDetails: Intervention physicians received near-real-time, individualized feedback on SEP-1 performance generated by a large language model (LLM) that performed automated chart abstraction at the time of ED discharge. Exact LLM model, prompt design, and feedback delivery format/frequency are not available in the accessible sources.
studyDetails.interventionDetails_ko: 중재군 의사는 ED 퇴원 시점에 자동 차트 추출을 수행하는 대규모 언어모델(LLM)이 생성한 거의 실시간 개인별 SEP-1 성과 피드백을 받았다. 정확한 LLM 모델, 프롬프트 설계, 피드백 전달 형식/빈도는 접근 가능한 자료에서 확인되지 않는다.
studyDetails.comparatorDetails: Control physicians received standard sepsis quality feedback processes without real-time augmentation; per the registry this feedback is much less frequent and typically delivered 3-4 months after the patient interaction.
studyDetails.comparatorDetails_ko: 대조군 의사는 실시간 보강 없이 표준 패혈증 품질 피드백 절차를 받았다; 등록부에 따르면 이 피드백은 빈도가 훨씬 낮고 일반적으로 환자 진료 후 3~4개월 뒤에 전달된다.
studyDetails.followUp: SEP-1 compliance assessed up to 1 month after the event; 30-day mortality was a secondary outcome (registry also lists in-hospital mortality up to 6 months after the event). Patient enrollment spanned December 13, 2024 to July 8, 2025.
studyDetails.followUp_ko: SEP-1 준수는 사건 후 최대 1개월까지 평가; 30일 사망률은 이차 결과였다(등록부에는 사건 후 최대 6개월까지의 원내 사망률도 기재). 환자 등록 기간은 2024년 12월 13일부터 2025년 7월 8일까지였다.
studyDetails.sourceBasis: Abstract (primary), PubMed metadata, ClinicalTrials.gov NCT07581340, and Crossref metadata. No PMCID was available for open full-text fetch, so detailed methods such as LLM specifics and baseline arm balance are limited.
studyDetails.sourceBasis_ko: 초록(주요), PubMed 메타데이터, ClinicalTrials.gov NCT07581340, Crossref 메타데이터. 공개 전문 확보용 PMCID가 없어 LLM 세부사항 및 기저 군간 균형 등 상세 방법은 제한적이다.

pico.population: Adult ED patients meeting CMS SEP-1 criteria for severe sepsis/septic shock, managed by ED attending physicians
pico.intervention: Near-real-time LLM-determined SEP-1 compliance feedback to physicians at patient discharge
pico.comparison: Standard (delayed, less frequent) sepsis quality feedback process
pico.outcome: Overall SEP-1 bundle compliance; ICU admission; 30-day mortality; LLM-expert agreement

pico_ko.population: CMS SEP-1 중증 패혈증/패혈성 쇼크 기준을 충족하고 ED 담당 전문의가 진료한 성인 응급실 환자
pico_ko.intervention: 환자 퇴원 시 의사에게 제공되는 거의 실시간 LLM 기반 SEP-1 준수 피드백
pico_ko.comparison: 표준(지연되고 빈도가 낮은) 패혈증 품질 피드백 절차
pico_ko.outcome: 전체 SEP-1 번들 준수율; ICU 입원; 30일 사망률; LLM-전문가 일치도

detailedPico.population: 66 ED attending physicians (cluster units) treating 301 SEP-1-eligible adults (121 control, 180 intervention); median age 64.3 years (IQR 51.1-75.7), 56.8% male, 17.3% chronic kidney disease, 17.3% chronic heart failure.
detailedPico.population_ko: 66명의 ED 담당 전문의(군집 단위)가 SEP-1 대상 성인 301명 치료(대조군 121명, 중재군 180명); 중위연령 64.3세(IQR 51.1-75.7), 남성 56.8%, 만성 신질환 17.3%, 만성 심부전 17.3%.
detailedPico.countrySetting: United States; 2 academic EDs within UC San Diego Health (NCT07581340).
detailedPico.countrySetting_ko: 미국; UC 샌디에이고 헬스 내 2개 학술 응급실(NCT07581340).
detailedPico.intervention: LLM-based automated medical record abstraction at ED discharge generating individualized, near-real-time SEP-1 compliance feedback to intervention-arm attending physicians.
detailedPico.intervention_ko: ED 퇴원 시 LLM 기반 자동 의무기록 추출로 중재군 담당 전문의에게 개인별 거의 실시간 SEP-1 준수 피드백 생성.
detailedPico.comparator: Standard sepsis quality feedback without real-time augmentation, delivered far less frequently and typically 3-4 months after the encounter.
detailedPico.comparator_ko: 실시간 보강 없는 표준 패혈증 품질 피드백으로, 빈도가 훨씬 낮고 일반적으로 진료 3~4개월 후 전달.

outcomes.label: Primary
outcomes.result: Control 70.1% vs intervention 82.9%; absolute improvement 13.0% (95% CI, 2.5%-23.4%); OR 2.10 (95% CI, 1.15-3.81); P = .02.
outcomes.statistics: Absolute difference 13.0% (95% CI 2.5%-23.4%); OR 2.10 (95% CI 1.15-3.81); P = .02 from mixed-effects logistic regression.
outcomes.interpretation: Physicians receiving real-time LLM feedback passed the SEP-1 bundle significantly more often, with roughly double the odds of compliance.
outcomes.interpretation_ko: 실시간 LLM 피드백을 받은 의사가 SEP-1 번들을 유의하게 더 자주 통과했으며 준수 오즈가 약 2배였다.
outcomes.statPrimer: OR 1 = no difference, >1 = more compliance with the intervention; CI 1.15-3.81 excludes 1 and P=.02 (<0.05), so the difference is statistically significant. The 95% CI for the absolute difference (2.5%-23.4%) excludes 0, confirming a real absolute gain.
outcomes.statPrimer_ko: OR 1 = 차이 없음, >1 = 중재군에서 준수 증가; CI 1.15-3.81이 1을 제외하고 P=.02(<0.05)이므로 통계적으로 유의하다. 절대 차이의 95% CI(2.5%-23.4%)가 0을 제외하여 실제 절대 이득을 확인한다.

outcomes.label: Secondary
outcomes.outcome: 30-mL/kg fluid bolus component noncompletion (largest component-level difference)
outcomes.outcome_ko: 30-mL/kg 수액 볼루스 구성요소 미완료(구성요소별 최대 차이)
outcomes.result: Intervention 3 of 180 (1.7%) vs control 16 of 121 (13.2%) noncompletion.
outcomes.result_ko: 중재군 180명 중 3명(1.7%) 대 대조군 121명 중 16명(13.2%) 미완료.
outcomes.statistics: Proportions only: 1.7% vs 13.2%; described as a documentation-sensitive component. No separate CI/P reported in the abstract.
outcomes.interpretation: Most of the improvement appeared in the fluid-bolus component, which the authors note is sensitive to documentation, suggesting better charting as much as changed care.
outcomes.interpretation_ko: 개선의 대부분은 수액 볼루스 구성요소에서 나타났으며, 저자들은 이것이 문서화에 민감하다고 언급해 실제 진료 변화만큼이나 문서화 개선이 기여했을 가능성을 시사한다.
outcomes.statPrimer: These are raw proportions; a lower percentage means fewer failures. No confidence interval or p value was provided for this component, so statistical significance cannot be formally judged from the available data.
outcomes.statPrimer_ko: 이는 원시 비율로, 낮은 비율은 실패가 적음을 의미한다. 이 구성요소에는 신뢰구간이나 p값이 제공되지 않아 가용 데이터로 통계적 유의성을 공식적으로 판단할 수 없다.

outcomes.label: Secondary
outcomes.outcome: Agreement between LLM SEP-1 determination and expert review
outcomes.outcome_ko: LLM SEP-1 판정과 전문가 검토 간 일치도
outcomes.statistics: Raw agreement 92%; no kappa or CI reported in the abstract.
outcomes.interpretation: The LLM matched expert SEP-1 adjudication in most cases, supporting feasibility of automated abstraction, though 8% disagreement remains clinically relevant.
outcomes.interpretation_ko: LLM은 대부분의 경우 전문가의 SEP-1 판정과 일치하여 자동 추출의 실현 가능성을 뒷받침하나, 8%의 불일치는 임상적으로 여전히 의미가 있다.
outcomes.statPrimer: Percent agreement is the share of cases where LLM and experts concur; higher is better, but raw agreement does not adjust for chance (no kappa reported).
outcomes.statPrimer_ko: 일치율은 LLM과 전문가가 동의한 사례의 비율로 높을수록 좋으나, 원시 일치율은 우연 일치를 보정하지 않는다(kappa 미보고).

outcomes.label: Secondary
outcomes.result: No significant differences between groups in ICU admissions or 30-day mortality.
outcomes.result_ko: ICU 입원 또는 30일 사망률에서 군간 유의한 차이 없음.
outcomes.statistics: Reported as no significant difference; exact event counts, effect estimates, CIs, and p values not available in the accessible sources.
outcomes.interpretation: The process improvement did not translate into measurable patient-centered outcome benefit, though the trial was not powered for mortality.
outcomes.interpretation_ko: 과정 지표 개선이 측정 가능한 환자 중심 결과 이득으로 이어지지 않았으나, 본 시험은 사망률에 대한 검정력이 없었다.
outcomes.statPrimer: When a study reports no significant difference (P>=0.05), it cannot confirm equivalence—especially in a small trial that may be underpowered to detect mortality differences.
outcomes.statPrimer_ko: 연구가 유의한 차이 없음(P>=0.05)을 보고할 때 이는 동등성을 확인하지 못하며, 특히 사망률 차이를 검출할 검정력이 부족할 수 있는 소규모 시험에서 그렇다.

keyFindings: Real-time LLM-driven SEP-1 feedback raised overall bundle compliance from 70.1% to 82.9% (absolute +13.0%, OR 2.10, P=.02).
The largest component-level gain was in the documentation-sensitive 30-mL/kg fluid bolus (noncompletion 1.7% intervention vs 13.2% control).
LLM SEP-1 determinations agreed with expert review 92% of the time, but there was no significant difference in ICU admissions or 30-day mortality.
keyFindings_ko: 실시간 LLM 기반 SEP-1 피드백은 전체 번들 준수율을 70.1%에서 82.9%로 높였다(절대 +13.0%, OR 2.10, P=.02).
구성요소별 최대 개선은 문서화에 민감한 30-mL/kg 수액 볼루스였다(미완료율 중재군 1.7% 대 대조군 13.2%).
LLM SEP-1 판정은 전문가 검토와 92% 일치했으나 ICU 입원이나 30일 사망률에는 유의한 차이가 없었다.
analyses.conclusion: In this single-health-system cluster randomized trial, AI/LLM-enabled near-real-time SEP-1 abstraction with targeted physician feedback significantly improved overall bundle compliance, driven substantially by the documentation-sensitive fluid bolus component, with high LLM-expert agreement but no detectable effect on ICU admission or 30-day mortality.
analyses.conclusion_ko: 단일 헬스 시스템 군집 무작위 시험에서 AI/LLM 기반 거의 실시간 SEP-1 추출과 표적 의사 피드백은 전체 번들 준수율을 유의하게 개선했으며, 이는 주로 문서화에 민감한 수액 볼루스 구성요소가 주도했다. LLM-전문가 일치도는 높았으나 ICU 입원이나 30일 사망률에 대한 영향은 확인되지 않았다.
analyses.clinicalTakeaway: AI-generated, near-real-time SEP-1 feedback can meaningfully boost measured bundle compliance and offers a feasible path to automate burdensome quality reporting, but clinicians should recognize that much of the gain may reflect improved documentation rather than changed bedside care, and patient outcomes were unchanged in this small single-system trial.
analyses.clinicalTakeaway_ko: AI가 생성하는 거의 실시간 SEP-1 피드백은 측정된 번들 준수율을 의미 있게 높이고 부담스러운 품질 보고 자동화의 실현 가능한 경로를 제시하지만, 임상의는 개선의 상당 부분이 실제 침상 진료 변화보다 문서화 개선을 반영할 수 있으며 이 소규모 단일 시스템 시험에서 환자 결과는 변하지 않았음을 인식해야 한다.
analyses.limitations: Single health system (2 UCSD academic EDs) limits generalizability; small sample (66 physician clusters, 301 patients) with unequal arm sizes (121 vs 180) and no available baseline balance data; the trial was not powered for mortality/ICU outcomes. The primary gain was concentrated in a documentation-sensitive component, raising the possibility that improved charting rather than improved care explains part of the effect. Masking was limited (single-blind, registry lists masking none), and detailed methods (LLM model, feedback format, statistical handling of clustering details) are not available without full text. Registry framing differs slightly from the article (e.g., in-hospital vs 30-day mortality).
analyses.limitations_ko: 단일 헬스 시스템(UCSD 2개 학술 응급실)으로 일반화 가능성이 제한됨; 소규모 표본(의사 군집 66명, 환자 301명)에 군간 크기 불균형(121 대 180)과 기저 균형 자료 부재; 사망률/ICU 결과에 대한 검정력 없음. 주요 이득이 문서화에 민감한 구성요소에 집중되어 진료 개선이 아닌 차트 작성 개선이 효과의 일부를 설명할 가능성이 있음. 맹검이 제한적(단일맹검, 등록부는 맹검 없음으로 기재)이고, 상세 방법(LLM 모델, 피드백 형식, 군집 처리 통계 세부사항)은 전문 없이는 확인 불가. 등록부 기술은 논문과 약간 다름(예: 원내 사망률 대 30일 사망률).
analyses.edIcuApplicability: Directly relevant to ED and critical care quality programs: the workflow targets ED-managed severe sepsis/septic shock and physician-level SEP-1 performance, a measure most EDs already track. The approach could automate sepsis quality auditing and accelerate feedback loops within a learning health system. However, EM/CCM clinicians should be cautious that the demonstrated benefit is a process measure, heavily influenced by documentation of the 30-mL/kg bolus, with no proven mortality or ICU benefit; external validation across diverse, non-academic EHRs and EDs is needed before broad adoption.
analyses.edIcuApplicability_ko: ED 및 중환자 품질 프로그램에 직접적으로 관련: 이 워크플로우는 ED에서 관리되는 중증 패혈증/패혈성 쇼크와 대부분의 ED가 이미 추적하는 의사 개인별 SEP-1 성과를 대상으로 한다. 이 접근법은 패혈증 품질 감사를 자동화하고 학습형 보건 시스템 내 피드백 루프를 가속화할 수 있다. 다만 EM/CCM 임상의는 입증된 이득이 30-mL/kg 볼루스 문서화에 크게 영향받는 과정 지표이며 사망률이나 ICU 이득이 입증되지 않았음을 유의해야 하고, 광범위한 도입 전 다양한 비학술 EHR 및 ED에서의 외부 검증이 필요하다.
analysisNotes: No PMCID available; analysis based on abstract plus PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT07581340), and Crossref metadata.
Registry enrollment of 66 refers to physician clusters, not patients; actual analyzed patient sample was 301 (121 control, 180 intervention).
Registry lists in-hospital mortality (up to 6 months) and masking none, while the article specifies 30-day mortality and single-blind design—minor discrepancies noted.
Exact event counts, CIs, and p values for ICU admission and 30-day mortality were not available in the accessible sources.
Authors flag the fluid-bolus component as documentation-sensitive, an important caveat when interpreting the compliance gain.

paper.title: Medical Record Abstraction for Quality Improvement in Sepsis Care Using Artificial Intelligence: A Cluster Randomized Trial.
paper.abstract:
IMPORTANCE: Hospital quality reporting remains a manual, costly process with critical limitations as a mechanism to improve care outcomes.
OBJECTIVE: To assess whether near-real-time quality measurement, enabled by large language models (LLMs), can improve quality performance as measured by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) Severe Sepsis and Septic Shock Management Bundle (SEP-1) quality metric.
DESIGN, SETTING, AND PARTICIPANTS: This single-blind, unstratified, cluster randomized trial was conducted between December 13, 2024, and July 8, 2025, at 2 academic emergency departments (EDs) within the University of California, San Diego (UCSD) health system. Participants included all 66 attending physicians who practiced in the UCSD EDs and worked more than 3 shifts per month prior to study initiation.
INTERVENTION: Participants were randomized to receive targeted feedback from LLM-determined compliance with SEP-1 at the time of patient discharge or standard process.
MAIN OUTCOMES AND MEASURES: The primary outcome was overall compliance with SEP-1. Secondary outcomes included expert agreement with the LLM SEP-1 determination, 30-day mortality, and intensive care unit admissions of patients with severe sepsis and/or septic shock in the ED. Effect sizes were estimated from a mixed-effects logistic regression model with the intervention group as a fixed effect and a random intercept for physician.
RESULTS: The study population included 66 physicians who treated 301 patients (121 in the control group and 180 in the intervention group; median age, 64.3 [IQR, 51.1-75.7] years; 171 [56.8%] male; 52 [17.3%] with chronic kidney disease; 52 [17.3%] with chronic heart failure) who met CMS inclusion criteria for SEP-1. Physicians in the control group had a SEP-1 compliance rate of 70.1%, while those in the intervention group had a rate of 82.9%. Assignment to the intervention group resulted in a 13.0% absolute improvement in SEP-1 compliance (95% CI, 2.5%-23.4%; odds ratio, 2.10 [95% CI, 1.15-3.81]; P = .02) in the mixed-effects model. The largest difference between the intervention group and control group was in noncompletion of the 30-mL/kg fluid bolus component (3 of 180 [1.7%] vs 16 of 121 [13.2%]), a documentation-sensitive component of the quality measure. Agreement between LLM determination and expert review was 92%. No significant differences existed in intensive care unit admissions or 30-day mortality.
CONCLUSIONS AND RELEVANCE: In this cluster randomized trial of artificial intelligence (AI)-enabled medical record abstraction for sepsis care, rapid assessment of SEP-1 performance and targeted feedback improved overall compliance with the measure. AI-driven quality clinical integration may address limitations in existing hospital quality reporting and better support a learning health system.
TRIAL REGISTRATION: ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT07581340.

Public trial registry from ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT07581340):
Brief title: Using AI to Improve Sepsis Quality of Care in the Emergency Department
Official title: Impact of Automated Sepsis Metric Evaluation on Provider Performance
Overall status: COMPLETED
Study type / phase: INTERVENTIONAL, NA
Design: RANDOMIZED, SINGLE_GROUP, OTHER, masking NONE
Enrollment: 66 (ACTUAL)
Countries: United States
Sample recruiting/participating locations:
- UC San Diego Health, San Diego, California, United States
Arms/groups:
- Intervention group (EXPERIMENTAL): Participants in the intervention arm receive near-real-time, individualized feedback on SEP-1 performance generated by a large language model (LLM) that performs automated chart abstraction at the time of emergency department discharge.
- Control group (NO_INTERVENTION): Participants in the control arm receive standard sepsis quality feedback processes without real-time augmentation. This is much less than the intervention group and typically 3-4 months after a particular interaction.
Interventions:
- Near-real time automated feedback on SEP-1 performance (BEHAVIORAL): Participants in the intervention arm receive near-real-time, individualized feedback on SEP-1 performance generated by a large language model (LLM) that performs automated chart abstraction at the time of emergency department discharge.
Primary outcomes:
- SEP-1 Compliance | time frame: This was assessed from time of the event (e.g., development of severe sepsis / septic shock) up to 1 month after the event. | SEP-1 (the Severe Sepsis and Septic Shock Early Management Bundle) is a quality measure developed by the Centers for Medicare \& Medicaid Services that evaluates whether patients with sepsis receive a set of time-sensitive interventions and diagnostic tests. It is a binary process measure (e.g., either a "pass" or "fail").
Secondary outcomes:
- In-hospital mortality | time frame: From when the patient arrives to the hospital to their discharge, either alive or expired, up to 6 months after the event (development of severe sepsis / septic shock). | Whether a patient survives or dies during a hospitalization.
Eligibility excerpt: Inclusion Criteria: * Adult patients (≥18 years) evaluated in the emergency department Clinical diagnosis of severe sepsis or septic shock during the emergency department encounter Cases meeting Centers for Medicare \& Medicaid Services (CMS) SEP-1 inclusion criteria Patient encounter managed by an attending emergency physician participating in the study Sepsis "time zero" occurring during the emergency department visit Exclusion Criteria: * Patients who do not meet CMS SEP-1 criteria for severe sepsis or septic shock Sepsis onset occurring prior to emergency department arrival or after hospital admission Encounters without sufficient clinical documentation to assess SEP-1 compliance Patients transferred from another facility with ongoing sepsis care already initiated Cases in which the attending physician of record is not assigned to a study arm

selected.rationale_ko: 응급의학 환경에서 수행된 클러스터 무작위배정 임상시험(JAMA Network Open)으로, 대형언어모델(LLM)을 활용한 준실시간 SEP-1 패혈증 번들 준수 피드백이 질 지표와 임상 결과(30일 사망률, 중환자실 입실 등)에 미치는 영향을 평가함. 패혈증은 EM/CCM 핵심 주제이며, AI 기반 질 측정 및 피드백은 실무 적용 가능성이 높고 시의성 있는 주제임. 후보군 중 유일하게 EM 현장 RCT이면서 결정론적 점수도 가장 높아(16점) 최우선 선정함. 다만 단일 의료시스템·소규모(의사 66명, N=301)라는 한계로 즉각적 진료 변경 근거로는 다소 약해 임상우선순위 점수는 중상위로 평가함.

notes_ko: 최우선 후보 PMID 42348212는 응급실 기반 클러스터 RCT로 패혈증 SEP-1 번들 준수에 대한 LLM 기반 실시간 피드백을 평가하여 EM/CCM 실무 관련성과 시의성이 가장 높음.
대안 후보로 PMID 42339854(균형수액 vs 생리식염수 체계적 고찰의 overview)와 PMID 42329792(독일 패혈증 병원간 전원 대규모 관찰연구, N=347826)가 임상 관련성이 있었으나, 전자는 신규 데이터가 아닌 기존 리뷰 종합이고 후자는 행정자료 기반 관찰연구로 직접적 진료 변경력이 약함.
다수 후보(PMID 42303390, 42311997, 42308517, 42315266, 42331582 등)는 연구 프로토콜 또는 타당성 연구로 결과가 없어 즉시 활용 가능한 임상 근거가 부족하여 제외함.
MIMIC-IV 기반 회고적 코호트(PMID 42332467, 42315260 일부, 42309977, 42304606)는 가설생성 수준의 관찰연구로 우선순위를 낮춤.
주제 무관(교도소 여성 건강 PMID 42331574, 만성기침 PMID 42319606, MST PTSD PMID 42313385 등)은 EM/CCM 직접 관련성이 낮아 배제함.
